hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in International Business: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with an International Business specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. International Trade and Finance, Global Marketing Management, Export Import Management, Cross-Cultural Management, and Global Business Strategy in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA International Business: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. IB depth with International Trade, Global Marketing, Export Import Management, Cross-Cultural Management, International HRM, and Global Business Strategy. Strong for export management, international sales, and global trade careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in International Business is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The International Business specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering International Trade and Finance, Global Marketing Management, Export Import Management, Cross-Cultural Management, International Human Resource Management, and Global Business Strategy. These subjects are designed for professionals targeting export management, international sales, global sourcing, and cross-border business development roles.

LPU's large NRI and international student community gives its International Business specialisation a distinctive advantage: students and alumni from multiple countries provide genuine cross-cultural interaction that enriches the programme's global business content. The zero-cost EMI makes this the most accessible NIRF-ranked International Business MBA in India.

sem3Subjects.name: International Trade and Finance
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse multilateral trade frameworks (WTO, FTAs), trade finance instruments, and balance of payments for international business roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Global Marketing Management
sem3Subjects.description: Adapt marketing strategy across international markets: cultural segmentation, global pricing, international distribution, and brand localisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Export Import Management
sem3Subjects.description: Manage export documentation, DGFT regulations, customs clearance procedures, and import duty optimisation for trade roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Design global business strategy: market entry mode selection, international expansion frameworks, and cross-border M&A analysis.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the International Business specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present international business summer project findings to faculty panel, developing cross-cultural business communication skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied international business project on market entry strategy, export development, or global operations design.

sem4Subjects.name: Cross-Cultural Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Hofstede and GLOBE cultural frameworks to cross-border team management, negotiation style adaptation, and global leadership.

sem4Subjects.name: International Human Resource Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design expatriate management policies, global mobility strategy, and cross-border talent acquisition for multinational HR roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Business Strategy
sem4Subjects.description: Develop global competitive positioning, international portfolio management, and multinational corporate strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the International Business specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
International Business MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Export-import trading houses, Multinational FMCG and consumer goods, IT services with global delivery, International logistics, Pharma exports, Global sourcing

**Entry-level roles:** International Business Executive, Export Documentation Executive, Global Sourcing Analyst, Trade Finance Analyst, International Sales Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** International Business Manager, Export Manager, Global Key Account Manager, Cross-Border Procurement Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP International Business, Head of Global Trade, Country Manager, International Operations Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Tata International, Mahindra Agri, Infosys global delivery, TCS international, EXIM Bank India, ICICI Bank Trade Finance, Maersk India, DHL Global, Punjab National Bank International, Hero MotoCorp International, Lupin Pharma International

skills.body:
The LPU International Business specialisation builds skills that export-oriented and multinational employers test at international business manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Export Import Management: DGFT regulation compliance, customs documentation management, import duty optimisation, and trade procedure management for export and trade roles
- International Trade and Finance: WTO and FTA framework application, trade finance instrument management, and balance of payments analysis for international banking and trade strategy roles
- Global Marketing Management: international market entry analysis, cultural adaptation of brand and pricing, and global distribution channel design
- International Human Resource Management: expatriate policy design, global mobility management, and cross-border talent strategy for multinational HR roles

**Applied skills:**
- Cross-Cultural Management: Hofstede framework application, cross-cultural negotiation style adaptation, and global team management
- Global Business Strategy: market entry mode evaluation, international portfolio management, and multinational competitive positioning

**Soft skills:** Negotiating cross-border contracts and trade agreements, presenting international market entry strategy to management, managing geographically distributed teams, and building joint venture partnerships across cultural contexts.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA International Business against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA International Business Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity International Business is a strong all-India brand with coverage of global strategy and international marketing. LPU International Business at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is significantly more affordable and adds dedicated Cross-Cultural Management and International HRM subjects. LPU offers zero-cost EMI; Amity does not. For North India export-import and international trade careers, LPU is a clear value proposition.

**vs SSODL Online MBA International Business:** SSODL (Symbiosis) is NAAC A++ and NIRF #24 at Rs 3,15,000-3,70,000 with interest-bearing EMI. SSODL International Business adds dedicated Trade Finance and FOREX Management and International Logistics subjects. For Pune-based MNC and western India trade careers, SSODL carries stronger brand recognition. For North India international business careers at significantly lower fees with zero-cost EMI, LPU International Business is the accessible alternative.

**vs MUJ Online MBA International Business:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000. MUJ International Business covers global business with Coursera Plus. LPU International Business includes dedicated Cross-Cultural Management, International HRM, and Global Business Strategy as distinct subjects. Both are comparable at similar fee levels.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in International Business offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The International Business specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA International Business alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Mandeep Gill
items.liked: Export Import Management and International Trade subjects were directly applicable to my export documentation role. Cross-Cultural Management was uniquely useful for managing my NRI client relationships.
items.disliked: Trade Finance content could be deeper with more letters of credit and FOREX hedging case studies.
items.body: I had 4 years in export documentation and the LPU International Business MBA helped me move to international business manager. The Rs 1.61L with zero-cost EMI was the deciding factor. LPU's international student community made the cross-cultural content genuine.

items.name: Sunita Negi
items.liked: Global Marketing Management and International HRM subjects were well-structured. Good for multinational HR and global marketing roles.
items.disliked: International Trade Finance subject depth could match SSODL's dedicated coverage.
items.body: Good programme for international business career advancement. Delhi-NCR multinationals recognised the LPU credential. The zero-cost EMI was genuinely beneficial.

items.name: Deepti Sharma
items.liked: Global Business Strategy and Cross-Cultural Management gave me strong frameworks for my MNC role.
items.disliked: Placement support for international roles was limited outside North India.
items.body: Strong international business content at an affordable price. For North India export-import and multinational careers, LPU is a recognised and cost-effective choice.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Is LPU International Business MBA useful for export documentation professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Export Import Management covers DGFT regulations, customs clearance, export documentation, and import duty optimisation directly relevant to export trade roles. Combined with International Trade and Finance and Global Business Strategy, the programme supports career progression from export documentation to international business management.

faqs.question: Does LPU have international students which helps with cross-cultural learning?
faqs.answer: Yes. LPU's on-campus and online programmes attract a significant international student base including students from African countries, Nepal, and overseas Indians. This international community enriches the Cross-Cultural Management and Global Business Strategy subjects with practical cross-cultural interaction.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You are in export-import, international sales, global sourcing, or multinational management roles and want an affordable credentialed MBA.
You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want dedicated Cross-Cultural Management, International HRM, and Global Business Strategy alongside Export Import Management.
consider: If Trade Finance, FOREX Management, and International Logistics depth is required, SSODL Online MBA International Business offers deeper coverage at Rs 3.15L.
If pan-India multinational brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA International Business Management at Rs 2.25L is well recognised.
If Manipal brand with Coursera Plus global business content is preferred, MUJ Online MBA International Business is available at comparable fees.
